# DataCoolie

> Metadata-driven ETL framework for Python — engine-unified, cloud-agnostic, batch-first.

DataCoolie lets data engineering teams describe pipeline intent once as metadata
(JSON / YAML / Excel / database / REST API) and execute on Polars, Spark, Fabric,
Databricks, or AWS Glue without rewriting per-engine code. Version: 0.1.0.

## Documentation

- [Home](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/): Overview, value proposition, and quickstart
- [Getting Started](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/getting-started/): Installation, Polars quickstart, Spark quickstart, first dataflow tutorial
- [Concepts](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/concepts/): Architecture, engines, platforms, metadata model, orchestration, logging, secrets, watermarks
- [How-to Guides](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/how-to/): Configuring metadata, running stages, merge/SCD2, partitioning, maintenance, deployment recipes
- [Reference](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/reference/): Metadata schema, plugin entry points, CLI, environment variables, full Python API
- [Extending](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/extending/): Plugin development — sources, destinations, transformers, engines, secret resolvers, metadata providers
- [Operations](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/operations/): Production guidance, logging layout, benchmarks, troubleshooting, testing strategy
- [ADR](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/adr/): Architecture decision records for engine format dispatch, secret provider split, transformer ordering, watermark contract
- [Changelog](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/changelog/): Version history and release notes

## Source Code

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/datacoolie/datacoolie): Source code, issues, and contributions

- [Reference — API Core](https://datacoolie.github.io/datacoolie/reference/api/core/)
